content_markdown:
# SoP6/24 – Solvency II regulatory reporting waivers

Statement of policy 6/24

First published on 29 February 2024

This statement of policy sets out the PRA's approach to waiving Solvency UK regulatory reporting requirements. The statement is addressed to all Solvency UK firms and to Lloyd’s, and should be read alongside all relevant EU legislation, and the Reporting Part of the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) Rulebook.

The statement:

  * lists the reporting covered by certain waivers and modifications by consents; and
  * explains the steps a firm must take to apply for waivers and modification by consents, and how the decision will be communicated to the firm.

## Current version

Published on 15 November 2024. Effective from 31 December 2024.

****
  * [SoP6/24 – Solvency II regulatory reporting waivers statement – November 2024 (PDF)](/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/statement-of-policy/2024/solvency-ii-regulatory-reporting-waivers-november-2024-update.pdf)

- Following [PS15/24 – Solvency II: Restatement of assimilated law](/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/november/review-of-solvency-ii-restatement-of-assimilated-law-policy-statement)

## Past version

### **Published on 4 June 2024. Effective from 31 December 2024 (superseded on 15 November 2024 by November 2024 version).**

****
  * [Statement of policy – Solvency II regulatory reporting waivers statement – June 2024 (PDF)](/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/solvency-ii-regulatory-reporting-waivers-sop-june-2024.pdf)

\- Following [PS3/24 – Review of Solvency II: Reporting and disclosure phase 2 near-final](/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/february/review-of-solvency-ii-reporting-disclosure-phase-2-near-final-policy-statement)

First publication of SS.

---

## Full Document Content (from BoE PDF)

Solvency   II   regulatory   reporting   waivers  Statement   of   policy   6/24  November   2024

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority  Solvency   II   regulatory   reporting   waivers  Statement   of   policy  November   2024

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   1  Contents  Contents   1  1:   Introduction   2  2:   Solvency   II   reporting   waivers   3  Reporting   by   individual   firms   3  Reporting   by   Category   3   and   4   third   country   branches   4  Application   for   Solvency   II   reporting   waiver   by   Category   1   and   2   firms,   and   insurance   groups  6  Annex   –   SoP   update   8

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   2  1:   Introduction  1.1   This statement of policy (SoP) sets out the Prudential Regulation Authority’s (PRA)  approach to waiving the submission of certain Solvency II reporting requirements in the  Reporting   Part   of   the   PRA   Rulebook,   using its   statutory   powers   under   section   138A   of   the  Financial Services and Markets Act (FSMA).  1.2   This   SoP   is   relevant   to   all   UK   Solvency   II   firms,   third   country   branch undertakings   and   the  Society of Lloyd’s, its members and managing agents. This SoP does not apply to Swiss  general insurers, as defined in the PRA Rulebook. It should be read alongside all relevant  onshored EU legislation, and the Reporting Part of the PRA Rulebook. 1  1.3   In   particular,   this   SoP:  •   lists   the   reporting   requirements applicable   to   individual   firms   and   third   country   branch  undertakings granted the waivers set out in this SoP; and  •   explains   the   steps   a   firm   must   take   to   apply   for   a waiver,   and   how   the   decision   will   be  communicated to the firm.  1.4   Firms should also refer to supervisory statement (SS) 2/19   —   PRA approach to  interpreting   reporting   and disclosure   requirements   and   regulatory   transactions   forms   after   EU  withdrawal. 2  1.5   Any   reference   to   any   provision   of   direct   EU   legislation   is   a   reference   to   it   as   it   forms   part  of retained EU law.  1   Paragraphs   2.1   to   2.4,   2.7   to   2.11   and   2.13   have   been   transferred   with   necessary   changes   from   SS11/15,  which   will   be   repealed   by   the   PRA   on   31   December   2024   and   transferred   to   this   SoP.   Paragraphs   2.5   and  2.6   have   been   transferred   with   necessary   changes   from   SS44/15.  2   December   2020: www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/publication/2019/pra - approach - to -  interpreting - reporting - and - disclosure - reqs - and - reg - trans - forms - ss.

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   3  2:   Solvency   II   reporting   waivers  2.1   The reporting requirements are set out in the Reporting Part of the PRA Rulebook. The  PRA considers that some firms may be eligible to apply for a modification by consent to  waive   regular   supervisory   reporting as   set   out   in   this   SoP,   unless   specifically   instructed   by   its  supervisory   team.   The   PRA   will   grant   this   waiver   via   a   rule   modification   under   s138A   FSMA. 3  2.1   A   Where   the   PRA   grants   a   firm   a   waiver,   as   provided   for   in   Reporting   4.1,   permitting   the  firm not to disclose information otherwise required to be disclosed in its SFCR, the PRA  expects that waiver shall remain in place, only for as long as the reason for non - disclosure  continues to exist.  Reporting   by   individual   firms  2.2   The PRA considers that firms designated as Category 3 and 4 by the PRA under the  potential   impact   framework,   whether an   individual   firm or   part   of   a   group,   are   eligible   to   apply  for this waiver from quarterly reporting.  2.3   Firms   that   are   granted the   waiver   from   reporting   certain quarterly   templates   for   individual  firms will only have to submit the reporting templates identified in Table A.  2.4   As   shown   in   Table   A,   the   reporting   waiver   at   the   individual   firm   level   does   not   apply   to  second quarter reporting of:  •   own   funds   and   balance   sheet   templates;   nor  •   the   basic   information   and   content   of   submission   templates.  The   latter   templates   will   help   the   PRA   understand   basic   information   about   each   submission  and facilitate validation checks.  3   While   the   term   ‘waiver’   is   used   throughout   this   document,   the   exemption   would   be   granted   by   a   modification  by consent.

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   4  Quarter   2  Basic   information   —   IR.01.02.01  Content   of   the   submission   —   IR.01.01.02  Balance   sheet   —   IR.02.01.02  Information   on   own   funds   —   IR.23.01.01  Reporting   by   Category   3   and   4   third   country   branches  2.5   The PRA considers that third country branch undertakings (other than pure reinsurance  branches)   designated   as   Category   3   and   4   by   the   PRA   under   the   potential   impact   framework,  are   eligible   to   apply   for   this waiver   from certain   aspects   of quarterly,   semi - annual,   and   annual  reporting. The PRA will grant this waiver via a rule modification under s138A FSMA. The  PRA may consider waivers for Category 1 and 2 third country branches on an exceptional  basis.  2.6   Firms   that are third   country branch undertakings   and   have been granted the third country  branch   reporting   waiver   will   only   have   to   submit   the   templates   identified   in Table   B   applicable  to them.  Table   B:   Aspects   of   reporting   required   of   third   country   branches   granted   a   reporting  waiver  Semi - annual   reporting   at   Quarter   2   and   Quarter   4  Content   of   the   submission   –   IR.01.01.08  Basic   information   –   general   –   IR.01.02.07  Balance   sheet   –   IR.02.01.08  Table   A:   Aspects   of   quarterly   reporting   required   of   individual   firms   granted   a   reporting  waiver

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   5  Table   B:   Aspects   of   reporting   required   of   third   country   branches   granted   a   reporting  waiver  Annual   reporting  Content   of   the   submission   –   IR.01.01.07  Basic   information   –   general   –   IR.01.02.07  Basic   information   –   RFF   and   matching   adjustment   portfolios   –   IR.01.03.01  Basic   information   –   branch   legal   entity   –   IR.01.04.07  Balance   sheet   –   IR.02.01.07  Additional   branch   balance   sheet   information   –   IR.02.03.07  Life   income   and   expenditure   –   IR.05.03.01  Non - life   income,   expenditure   and   business   model   analysis   –   IR.05.04.07  Business   model   analysis   –   financial   guarantee   insurers   –   IR.05.07.01  Material   pooling   arrangements   –   IR.05.08.01  Assessable   mutuals   –   IR.05.09.01  Life   technical   provisions   –   IR.12.01.01  With - profits   value   of   bonus   –   IR.12.05.01  With - profits   liabilities   and   assets   –   IR.12.06.01  Life   obligations   analysis   –   IR.14.01.01  Non - life   annuities   information   –   IR.16.01.01  Non - life   technical   provisions   –   IR.17.01.01  Non - life   liability   projection   of   future   cash   flows   –   IR.18.02.01  Non - life   claim   development   (general   liability   sub - classes)   –   IR.19.02.01  Cyber   underwriting   risk   –   IR.21.04.01  Reinsurer   and   collateral   provider   entity   information   –   IR.30.05.01  Outwards   reinsurance   balance   sheet   exposures   –   IR.31.01.01  Content   of   the   submission   –   IRR.01.01.07  Life   technical   provisions   –   IRR.12.01.01  Non - life   technical   provisions   –   IRR.17.01.01

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   6  2.7   The PRA may, where necessary to meet its objectives, make ad hoc requests for  reporting   subject   to   a   waiver.   Firms   granted   a   waiver   from   reporting   should   therefore   maintain  their ability to respond to such ad hoc requests.  2.8   Should   a   waiver   be   revoked   by   the   PRA,   the   PRA   expects   that   firms   will   resume   reporting  within six months from the initial notification, unless there are specific circumstances where  such a delay would not be appropriate.  2.9   Should   a   waiver   expire   and   not   be   renewed   by   the   firm   or   the   PRA,   reporting   should  resume at the next scheduled reporting date.  Application   for   Solvency   II   reporting   waiver   by   Category   1   and  2 firms, and insurance groups  2.10   The PRA may consider waivers for Category 1 and 2 firms on an exceptional case - by -  case   basis.   These   cases   could   include   small   firms   that   are   part   of   groups,   where   the   group   is  designated as Category 1 or 2 by the PRA. Category 1 or 2 individual firms that believe they  are eligible for waiver of quarterly reporting should discuss this waiver with their supervisor  prior to submitting a formal application. An application should then be submitted by  completing the relevant application material published on the Bank of England (the Bank)  website. 4  2.11   The PRA has determined that groups subject to group supervision by the PRA in  accordance   with   the   Group   Supervision   Part   of   the   Rulebook   and   the   Solvency II   Regulations  2015 may be eligible for this waiver from quarterly reporting at the level of the group, on a  case - by - case basis. Groups that are granted this waiver may not be required to submit any  group level quarterly reporting templates. Groups subject to group supervision by the PRA  that believe they are eligible for waiver of quarterly reporting at the level of the group should  discuss this waiver with their group supervisor prior to submitting a formal application. An  application should then be submitted by completing the relevant application material  published on the Bank’s website. The timing of decisions on the applications for individual  firm and group level reporting may differ.  2.12   Category 1 or 2 third country branches that believe they are eligible for a waiver of  quarterly,   semi - annual,   and   annual   reporting   should   discuss   this with   their   supervisor   prior   to  submitting a formal application. An application should then be submitted by completing the  relevant questionnaire published on the Bank’s website.  4   www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/authorisations/waivers - and - modifications - of - rules .

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   7  2.13   Once the PRA   has assessed an application it will inform the firm of its decision. Where  an   application   is   approved,   the   firm   will   be   sent   a   Direction   letter   that   sets   out   the   duration   of  the waiver.  2.14   The   PRA   expects   firms   to   have   a   contingency   plan   in   place   in   case   their   application   is  rejected and maintain contingency plans in case the waiver should expire without being  extended. The PRA expects all firms to notify their supervisory contact as soon as they  believe there are any circumstances which may impact their suitability to hold the waiver.

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   8  Annex   –   SoP   update  This   annex   details   the   changes   that   have   been   made   to   this   SoP   following   its   initial  publication in 2024:  2024  November  Following   the   publication   of policy   statement   (PS)   15/24   –   Review   of   Solvency:   Restatement  of assimilated law 5   on 15 November 2024, the PRA inserted paragraph 2.1A to set out its  expectations of non - disclosure regarding a waiver granted to a firm.  5   November   2024:   www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/publication/2024/november/review -  of - solvency - ii - restatement - of - assimilated - law - policy - statement .
content_html:
SoP6/24 – Solvency II regulatory reporting waivers

Statement of policy 6/24

First published on 29 February 2024

This statement of policy sets out the PRA's approach to waiving Solvency UK regulatory reporting requirements. The statement is addressed to all Solvency UK firms and to Lloyd’s, and should be read alongside all relevant EU legislation, and the Reporting Part of the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) Rulebook.

The statement:

  • lists the reporting covered by certain waivers and modifications by consents; and
  • explains the steps a firm must take to apply for waivers and modification by consents, and how the decision will be communicated to the firm.  

Current version

  Published on 15 November 2024. Effective from 31 December 2024.
  • SoP6/24 – Solvency II regulatory reporting waivers statement – November 2024 (PDF)

- Following PS15/24 – Solvency II: Restatement of assimilated law

Past version

Published on 4 June 2024. Effective from 31 December 2024 (superseded on 15 November 2024 by November 2024 version).

  • Statement of policy – Solvency II regulatory reporting waivers statement – June 2024 (PDF)

- Following PS3/24 – Review of Solvency II: Reporting and disclosure phase 2 near-final

First publication of SS.

1:   Introduction  1.1   This   s tatement of   p olicy (SoP) sets out the   Prudential Regulation Authority’s (PRA)  approach   to   waiving   the submission of certain Solvency   II   reporting requirements in the  Reporting Part of the PRA Rulebook, using   its statutory powers under s ection 138A of the  Financial Services and Markets Act (FSMA) .  1.2   This   SoP is   relevant   to all   UK   Solvency   II   firms , th ird country branch   undertakings   and the  Society of Lloyd’s , its   members and managing agents .   This SoP does not apply to Swiss  g eneral   i nsurers, as defined in the PRA Rulebook .   It should be read alongside all relevant  onshored EU legislation, and the Report ing Part of the   PRA Rulebook. 1  1.3   In particular, this   SoP :  •   lists the reporting requirements   applicable to individual firms and third country branch  undertakings   granted the w aiver s   set out in this SoP ; and  •   explains the steps a firm must take to apply for a   waiver , and how the decision will be  communicated to the firm.  1.4   Firms should also refer to   s upervisory   s tatement (SS) 2/19   –   PRA approach to  interpreting reporting and disclosure requirements and regula tory transactions forms after EU  withdrawal 2   and   the E uropean   I nsurance and   O ccupational   P ensions   A uthority   (EIOPA)  Reporting and Disclosure Guidelines . 3  1.5   Any reference to any provision of direct EU legislation is a reference to it as it forms part  of   retained EU law.  1   Paragraphs 2.1 to 2. 4 , 2. 7   to 2. 11   and 2.1 3   have been transferred   with necessary changes   from SS11/15,  which   will be   repealed by the PRA on 31 December 2024 and transferred to this SoP. Paragraphs 2. 5 and  2.6   have been transferred   with necessary changes   from SS44/15.  2   December 2020: SS2/19   –   www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/publication/2019/pra -  approach - to - interpreting - reporting - and - disclos ure - reqs - and - reg - trans - forms - ss .  3   www.bankofengland.co.uk/ - /media/boe/files/paper/2020/december/gl - reporting - public - disclosure.pdf .  These   are derived from the EIOPA Guidelines as at 31 December 2020. This SoP is effective from 31 December 2024 and was published as part of PS3/24, and subsequently updated in June 2024. Please see: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/february/review-of-solvency-ii-reporting-disclosure-phase-2-near-final-policy-statement Effective from 31 December 2024

2:   Solvency   II   reporting   waiver s  2 .1   The reporting requirements are set out in the Reporting Part of the PRA Rulebook.   The  PRA considers that some firms may be eligible to apply for a modification by consent to  waive   regular supervisory reporting   as set out in this SoP ,   unless specifically instructed by its  supervisory team.   The PRA will grant this   waiver   via a rule modifi cation under s138A FSMA. 4  Reporting by individual firms  2.2   The PRA considers that firms designated as Category 3   and   4 by the PRA under the  potential impact framework, whether   an individual firm   or   part of a group,   are eligible to apply  for this   waiver   from   quarterly   reporting.  2. 3   Firms   that are granted   the   waiver   from reporting certain   quarterly templates for individual  firms   will only have to submit the   reporting templates   identified in Table A.  2. 4   As shown in   T able   A , the   reporting waiver   at the   individual firm   level does not apply to  second quarter reporting of :  •   own funds and balance sheet templates;   nor  •   the basic information and content of submission templates.  The latter templates will help the PRA understand basic information about ea ch submission  and facilitate validation checks.  Table A:   Aspects of   quarterly   r eporting   required of   individual   firms granted a   reporting  waiver  4   While the term ‘waiver’ is used throughout this document, the exemption would be granted by a modification  by consent. Please see: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/february/review-of-solvency-ii-reporting-disclosure-phase-2-near-final-policy-statement Effective from 31 December 2024

Reporting by   Category 3 and 4   third country branches  2 . 5   The PRA considers that third country branch   undertakings   (other than pure reinsurance  branches)   designated as Category 3 and 4 by the PRA under the potential impact framework,  are eligible to apply for this   waiver   from   certain aspects of   quarterly, semi - annual ,   and annual  reporting .   The PRA will grant this   waiver   via a rule modification under s138A FSMA.   T he  PRA may consider   waiver s for Category 1 and 2 third country branches on an exceptional  basis.  2. 6   Firms that are   third country   branch   undertakings and have been   granted the third country  branch   reporting waiv er   will only have to submit the templates identified in   T able B   applicable  to them . Please see: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/february/review-of-solvency-ii-reporting-disclosure-phase-2-near-final-policy-statement Effective from 31 December 2024

Table B:   Aspects of r eporting required of third country branches granted a reporting  waiver  Annual reporting  Content of the submission   –   IR .0 1 .01.07  Basic   information   –   general   –   IR .0 1 .02.07  Basic information   –   RFF and matching adjustment portfolios   –   IR.01.03.01  Basic information   –   branch legal entity   –   IR .01.04.07  Balance sheet   –   IR .02.01.07  Additional branch balance sheet information   –   IR .02.03.07  Life income and expenditure   –   IR .05.03.0 1  Non - life income, expenditure and business model analysis   –   IR .05.0 4 .07  Business model analysis   –   financial guarantee insurers   –   IR .05.0 7 .01  Material pooling arrangements   –   IR. 05.0 8 .01  Assessable mutuals   –   IR .05.0 9 .01  Life technical provisions   –   IR .12.01.01  With - profits value of bonus   –   IR .12.0 5 .01  With - profits liabilities and assets   –   IR .12.0 6 .01  Life obligations analysis   –   IR .14.01.01  Non - life annuities information   –   IR .16.01.01  Non - life technical provisions   –   IR .17.01.01  Non - life liability projection of future cash flows   –   IR .18.02.01  Non - life claim development   (general liability sub - classes)   –   IR .19.02.01  Cyber underwriting risk   –   IR . 21 .0 4 .01  Reinsurer and   collateral provider entity information   –   IR .30.05.01  Outwards reinsurance balance sheet exposures   –   IR .31.01.01  Content of the submission   –   IRR .0 1 .01.07  Life technical provisions   –   IRR .12.01.01  Non - life technical provisions   –   IRR .17.01.01 This SoP is effective from 31 December 2024 and was published as part of PS3/24, and subsequently updated in June 2024. 2. 7   The PRA may, where necessary to meet its objectives, make ad hoc requests for  reporting   subject   to a waiver . Firms granted a   waiver   from reporting should therefore maintain  their ability to respond to such ad hoc requests.  2. 8   Should a   waiver   be revoked by   the PRA, the PRA expects that firms will resume reporting  within six months from the initial notification, unless there are specific circumstances where  such a delay would not be appropriate.  2. 9   Should a   waiver   expire and not be renewed by the firm or t he PRA, reporting should  resume at the next scheduled reporting date.  Application for Solvency   II   reporting   waiver   by Category 1 and  2   firm s, and insurance groups  2.1 0   The   PRA may consider   waivers   for Category 1 and 2 firms on an exceptional   case - by -  case b asis . These cases could include small firms that are part of groups, where the group is  designated as   C ategory 1 or 2 by the PRA.   Category 1 or 2   individual   firms   that believe they  are eligible for   waiver   of   quarterly reporting should discuss this   waiver   w ith their supervisor  prior to submitting a formal application.   An application should then be submitted by  completing the relevant   application material   published on the Bank of England   (the Bank)  website. 5  2. 11   The PRA has determined that groups subject to group supervision by the PRA   in  accordance with the Group Supervision Part of the Rulebook and the Solvency   II   Regulations  2015   may be eligible for this   waiver   from quarterly reporting at the level of the grou p, on a  case - by - case basis. Groups that are granted this   waiver   may not be required to submit any  group level quarterly reporting templates.   G roups subject to group supervision by the PRA  that believe they are eligible for   waiver   of   quarterly reporting at   the level of the group should  discuss this   waiver   with their group supervisor prior to submitting a formal application. An  application should then be submitted by completing the relevant   application material  published on the Bank ’s   website. The timing of   decisions on the applications   for individual  firm and group level reporting   may differ.  2.1 2   Category 1 or 2 third country branches that believe they are eligible for   a   waiver   o f  quarterly , semi - annual ,   and annual reporting should discuss this   with   their supervisor prior to  submitting a formal application. An application should then be submitted by completing the  relevant questionnaire published on the Bank ’s   website.  5   See   www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/authorisations/waivers - and - modifications - of -  rules . This SoP is effective from 31 December 2024 and was published as part of PS3/24, and subsequently updated in June 2024. Please see: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/february/review-of-solvency-ii-reporting-disclosure-phase-2-near-final-policy-statement Effective from 31 December 2024

2.1 3   Once the PRA has assessed an application it will inform the firm of its decision. Where  an application is approved, the firm will be sent a Direction letter that sets out the duration of  the waiver.  2.14   The PRA expects firms to have a contingency plan in   place in case their application is  rejected and maintain contingency plans in case the waiver should expire without being  extended. The PRA expects all firms to notify their supervisory contact as soon as they  believe there are any circumstances which may   impact their suitability to hold   the   waiver. This SoP is effective from 31 December 2024 and was published as part of PS3/24, and subsequently updated in June 2024. Please see: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential-regulation/publication/2024/february/review-of-solvency-ii-reporting-disclosure-phase-2-near-final-policy-statement Effective from 31 December 2024

Solvency   II   regulatory   reporting   waivers  Statement   of   policy   6/24  November   2024

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority  Solvency   II   regulatory   reporting   waivers  Statement   of   policy  November   2024

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   2  1:   Introduction  1.1   This statement of policy (SoP) sets out the Prudential Regulation Authority’s (PRA)  approach to waiving the submission of certain Solvency II reporting requirements in the  Reporting   Part   of   the   PRA   Rulebook,   using its   statutory   powers   under   section   138A   of   the  Financial Services and Markets Act (FSMA).  1.2   This   SoP   is   relevant   to   all   UK   Solvency   II   firms,   third   country   branch undertakings   and   the  Society of Lloyd’s, its members and managing agents. This SoP does not apply to Swiss  general insurers, as defined in the PRA Rulebook. It should be read alongside all relevant  onshored EU legislation, and the Reporting Part of the PRA Rulebook. 1  1.3   In   particular,   this   SoP:  •   lists   the   reporting   requirements applicable   to   individual   firms   and   third   country   branch  undertakings granted the waivers set out in this SoP; and  •   explains   the   steps   a   firm   must   take   to   apply   for   a waiver,   and   how   the   decision   will   be  communicated to the firm.  1.4   Firms should also refer to supervisory statement (SS) 2/19   —   PRA approach to  interpreting   reporting   and disclosure   requirements   and   regulatory   transactions   forms   after   EU  withdrawal. 2  1.5   Any   reference   to   any   provision   of   direct   EU   legislation   is   a   reference   to   it   as   it   forms   part  of retained EU law.  1   Paragraphs   2.1   to   2.4,   2.7   to   2.11   and   2.13   have   been   transferred   with   necessary   changes   from   SS11/15,  which   will   be   repealed   by   the   PRA   on   31   December   2024   and   transferred   to   this   SoP.   Paragraphs   2.5   and  2.6   have   been   transferred   with   necessary   changes   from   SS44/15.  2   December   2020: www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/publication/2019/pra - approach - to -  interpreting - reporting - and - disclosure - reqs - and - reg - trans - forms - ss.

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   3  2:   Solvency   II   reporting   waivers  2.1   The reporting requirements are set out in the Reporting Part of the PRA Rulebook. The  PRA considers that some firms may be eligible to apply for a modification by consent to  waive   regular   supervisory   reporting as   set   out   in   this   SoP,   unless   specifically   instructed   by   its  supervisory   team.   The   PRA   will   grant   this   waiver   via   a   rule   modification   under   s138A   FSMA. 3  2.1   A   Where   the   PRA   grants   a   firm   a   waiver,   as   provided   for   in   Reporting   4.1,   permitting   the  firm not to disclose information otherwise required to be disclosed in its SFCR, the PRA  expects that waiver shall remain in place, only for as long as the reason for non - disclosure  continues to exist.  Reporting   by   individual   firms  2.2   The PRA considers that firms designated as Category 3 and 4 by the PRA under the  potential   impact   framework,   whether an   individual   firm or   part   of   a   group,   are   eligible   to   apply  for this waiver from quarterly reporting.  2.3   Firms   that   are   granted the   waiver   from   reporting   certain quarterly   templates   for   individual  firms will only have to submit the reporting templates identified in Table A.  2.4   As   shown   in   Table   A,   the   reporting   waiver   at   the   individual   firm   level   does   not   apply   to  second quarter reporting of:  •   own   funds   and   balance   sheet   templates;   nor  •   the   basic   information   and   content   of   submission   templates.  The   latter   templates   will   help   the   PRA   understand   basic   information   about   each   submission  and facilitate validation checks.  3   While   the   term   ‘waiver’   is   used   throughout   this   document,   the   exemption   would   be   granted   by   a   modification  by consent.

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   4  Quarter   2  Basic   information   —   IR.01.02.01  Content   of   the   submission   —   IR.01.01.02  Balance   sheet   —   IR.02.01.02  Information   on   own   funds   —   IR.23.01.01  Reporting   by   Category   3   and   4   third   country   branches  2.5   The PRA considers that third country branch undertakings (other than pure reinsurance  branches)   designated   as   Category   3   and   4   by   the   PRA   under   the   potential   impact   framework,  are   eligible   to   apply   for   this waiver   from certain   aspects   of quarterly,   semi - annual,   and   annual  reporting. The PRA will grant this waiver via a rule modification under s138A FSMA. The  PRA may consider waivers for Category 1 and 2 third country branches on an exceptional  basis.  2.6   Firms   that are third   country branch undertakings   and   have been granted the third country  branch   reporting   waiver   will   only   have   to   submit   the   templates   identified   in Table   B   applicable  to them. Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   5  Table   B:   Aspects   of   reporting   required   of   third   country   branches   granted   a   reporting  waiver  Annual   reporting  Content   of   the   submission   –   IR.01.01.07  Basic   information   –   general   –   IR.01.02.07  Basic   information   –   RFF   and   matching   adjustment   portfolios   –   IR.01.03.01  Basic   information   –   branch   legal   entity   –   IR.01.04.07  Balance   sheet   –   IR.02.01.07  Additional   branch   balance   sheet   information   –   IR.02.03.07  Life   income   and   expenditure   –   IR.05.03.01  Non - life   income,   expenditure   and   business   model   analysis   –   IR.05.04.07  Business   model   analysis   –   financial   guarantee   insurers   –   IR.05.07.01  Material   pooling   arrangements   –   IR.05.08.01  Assessable   mutuals   –   IR.05.09.01  Life   technical   provisions   –   IR.12.01.01  With - profits   value   of   bonus   –   IR.12.05.01  With - profits   liabilities   and   assets   –   IR.12.06.01  Life   obligations   analysis   –   IR.14.01.01  Non - life   annuities   information   –   IR.16.01.01  Non - life   technical   provisions   –   IR.17.01.01  Non - life   liability   projection   of   future   cash   flows   –   IR.18.02.01  Non - life   claim   development   (general   liability   sub - classes)   –   IR.19.02.01  Cyber   underwriting   risk   –   IR.21.04.01  Reinsurer   and   collateral   provider   entity   information   –   IR.30.05.01  Outwards   reinsurance   balance   sheet   exposures   –   IR.31.01.01  Content   of   the   submission   –   IRR.01.01.07  Life   technical   provisions   –   IRR.12.01.01  Non - life   technical   provisions   –   IRR.17.01.01

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   6  2.7   The PRA may, where necessary to meet its objectives, make ad hoc requests for  reporting   subject   to   a   waiver.   Firms   granted   a   waiver   from   reporting   should   therefore   maintain  their ability to respond to such ad hoc requests.  2.8   Should   a   waiver   be   revoked   by   the   PRA,   the   PRA   expects   that   firms   will   resume   reporting  within six months from the initial notification, unless there are specific circumstances where  such a delay would not be appropriate.  2.9   Should   a   waiver   expire   and   not   be   renewed   by   the   firm   or   the   PRA,   reporting   should  resume at the next scheduled reporting date.  Application   for   Solvency   II   reporting   waiver   by   Category   1   and  2 firms, and insurance groups  2.10   The PRA may consider waivers for Category 1 and 2 firms on an exceptional case - by -  case   basis.   These   cases   could   include   small   firms   that   are   part   of   groups,   where   the   group   is  designated as Category 1 or 2 by the PRA. Category 1 or 2 individual firms that believe they  are eligible for waiver of quarterly reporting should discuss this waiver with their supervisor  prior to submitting a formal application. An application should then be submitted by  completing the relevant application material published on the Bank of England (the Bank)  website. 4  2.11   The PRA has determined that groups subject to group supervision by the PRA in  accordance   with   the   Group   Supervision   Part   of   the   Rulebook   and   the   Solvency II   Regulations  2015 may be eligible for this waiver from quarterly reporting at the level of the group, on a  case - by - case basis. Groups that are granted this waiver may not be required to submit any  group level quarterly reporting templates. Groups subject to group supervision by the PRA  that believe they are eligible for waiver of quarterly reporting at the level of the group should  discuss this waiver with their group supervisor prior to submitting a formal application. An  application should then be submitted by completing the relevant application material  published on the Bank’s website. The timing of decisions on the applications for individual  firm and group level reporting may differ.  2.12   Category 1 or 2 third country branches that believe they are eligible for a waiver of  quarterly,   semi - annual,   and   annual   reporting   should   discuss   this with   their   supervisor   prior   to  submitting a formal application. An application should then be submitted by completing the  relevant questionnaire published on the Bank’s website.  4   www.bankofengland.co.uk/prudential - regulation/authorisations/waivers - and - modifications - of - rules .

Bank   of   England   |   Prudential   Regulation   Authority   Page   7  2.13   Once the PRA   has assessed an application it will inform the firm of its decision. Where  an   application   is   approved,   the   firm   will   be   sent   a   Direction   letter   that   sets   out   the   duration   of  the waiver.  2.14   The   PRA   expects   firms   to   have   a   contingency   plan   in   place   in   case   their   application   is  rejected and maintain contingency plans in case the waiver should expire without being  extended. The PRA expects all firms to notify their supervisory contact as soon as they  believe there are any circumstances which may impact their suitability to hold the waiver.
